title: Common Agentic Mistakes, Restated
concept: Monolithic agents, missing sandboxes, ungoverned tool execution, and unmonitored loops.

exercises.prompt: What is a full-stack system designed to do?
exercises.explain: A full-stack system is designed to manage AI tools efficiently, integrating various components to enable smooth operations.

cards.title: Common Agentic Mistakes
cards.body: Agents can make errors that impact their effectiveness. Let's take a look at some common mistakes and how to avoid them.

cards.title: Monolithic Agents
cards.body: A monolithic agent is a single unit that handles all tasks without division. This can lead to inefficiencies and risks.

cards.title: Toolbox vs. Monolithic Machine
cards.body: Think of a toolbox full of specialized tools versus a single machine. Using the right tool for each job is often better than having one machine try to do everything.

cards.title: Missing Sandboxes
cards.body: A missing sandbox means the agent operates in an uncontrolled environment, leading to potential errors and unintended consequences. It’s like a child playing with toys unsupervised.

cards.title: Ungoverned Tool Execution is Safe
cards.body: This is a myth! Ungoverned execution can lead to harmful outcomes, much like letting a child use dangerous tools without guidance.

cards.title: Controlled vs. Uncontrolled Execution
cards.body: Controlled environments allow for safe operation of AI tools, unlike uncontrolled environments which can lead to mistakes.

exercises.prompt: What is a common mistake with monolithic agents?

options.text: They can perform all tasks efficiently.
They may fail to manage specific tasks well.
They require multiple sandboxes to function.
They are always the best choice for any situation.

exercises.explain: Monolithic agents may not manage specific tasks well since they lack the specialization that other types of agents offer.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Missing sandboxes are safe because they allow freedom for agents to operate.
exercises.explain: Missing sandboxes are not safe; they can lead to uncontrolled operations and unintended consequences.

exercises.prompt: Sandboxing prevents the agent from executing actions in an __________ environment.
exercises.sentence: Sandboxing prevents the agent from executing actions in an uncontrolled environment.
exercises.explain: Sandboxing keeps the agent safe by controlling the environment in which it operates.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements is AI-generated?

options.text: A monitored loop can enhance agent performance.
All agents should be monolithic for simplicity.
Sandboxes reduce the risk of errors.

exercises.explain: The statement that ‘all agents should be monolithic for simplicity’ is AI-generated and incorrect, as not all tasks benefit from a monolithic structure.

exercises.prompt: What is the best practice to avoid common agentic mistakes?

options.text: Ensure agents use sandboxes and specialized tasks.
Create all agents as monolithic for consistency.

exercises.explain: Using sandboxes and ensuring that agents are designed for specialized tasks helps reduce risks and errors, making the system more reliable.
